a:T176d8,<div class="specification">
<p>Cultivated rice, <em>Oryza sativa</em>, is one of the most important human foods. The two main sub-species of cultivated rice are <em>O. sativa indica</em> and <em>O. sativa japonica</em>. <em>O. rufipogon</em> is a wild species from which they probably evolved.</p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>To investigate whether the sub-species of cultivated rice evolved independently from the wild species, scientists analysed their chromosomes to find areas with similar DNA base sequences. Wild species tend to have many different alleles of genes present on each chromosome, but during the development of a crop plant by artificial selection from a wild species, this diversity drops considerably, increasing the base sequence similarity.<br/>The number of different alleles of the genes on a chromosome can be represented by a diversity index. The following graph of part of chromosome 7 shows the diversity index of <em>O. rufipogon</em> and two varieties of cultivated rice, <em>O. sativa indica</em> and <em>O. sativa japonica</em>. The gene PROG1 allows the plant to stand upright, which is typical of cultivated rice. Its position is indicated by the vertical arrow on the graph.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: He Z, Zhai W, Wen H, Tang T, Wang Y, Lu X, <em>et al</em>. (2011) Two Evolutionary Histories in the Genome of Rice:<br/>the Roles of Domestication Genes. <em>PLoS Genet</em> <strong>7</strong>(6): e1002100. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pgen.1002100]</p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p>In another experiment, scientists retrieved genome sequences of the wild rice <em>O. rufipogon</em> taken from a wide range of geographical sites (I, II and III) and those of the two sub-species of <em>O. sativa</em> from gene banks.</p>
<p>The pie charts, presented along with a cladogram, show the proportion of alleles for three genes which confer specific characteristics to <em>O. sativa</em>. Mutations can produce derived alleles that are different from the original ancestral alleles. The control group represents wild rice species other than <em>O. rufipogon</em>.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: reprinted by permission from Springer Nature: Nature, Huang, X., Kurata, N., Wei, X. <em>et al</em>. A map of rice<br/>genome variation reveals the origin of cultivated rice. <em>Nature</em> <strong>490</strong>, 497–501 (2012) doi:10.1038/nature11532]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the reason that <em>O. sativa</em> and <em>O. rufipogon</em> are classified as different species.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Determine which type of rice has the lowest overall diversity index.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Compare and contrast the trends for <em>O. rufipogon</em> and <em>O. sativa indica</em>.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State the proportion of the ancestral allele for the gene GS3 in the <em>O. rufipogon-III</em> population.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Distinguish between the proportion of ancestral and derived alleles for all three genes in <em>O. sativa indica</em> and <em>O. sativa japonica</em>.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">e.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State <strong>one</strong> reason for having a control group. </p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">f.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Using <strong>all</strong> of the data, discuss whether there is evidence that the two sub-species of <em>O. sativa</em> might have evolved independently from <em>O. rufipogon</em>.</p>
<div class="marks">[4]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">g.</div>
</div>b:T1034,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>cannot interbreed to produce <span style="text-decoration: underline;">fertile</span> offspring ✔</p>
<p><em>Accept converse or a good explanation</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>«<em>O. sativa</em>» <em>japonica</em> ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><em>Similarities</em><br/>both show diversity<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>similar pattern/peaks and troughs in the first part of the chromosome / up to «approximately» 1.5 megabases<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>similar diversity between 2.4 to 2.7 mb<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>both highest at 0.7 mb ✔</p>
<p><em>Differences</em><br/>there are «two» major drops in diversity for <em>O. sativa indica</em> whereas none for <em>O. rufipogon</em>/much wider fluctuations in <em>O. s. indica</em><br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/><em>O.s. indica</em> much lower at PROG1<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/><em>O.rufipogon</em> does not drop &lt; 2.5 whereas <em>O. s. indica</em> approaches 0<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/><em>O.rufipogon</em> generally higher than <em>O. s. indica</em> after 1.4-1.5 ✔</p>
<p><em>One answer from mpa and one from mpb required for 2 [max]</em>.</p>
<p><em>Accept a statement of where the drops occur.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{3}{4}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"> <mfrac> <mn>3</mn> <mn>4</mn> </mfrac> </math></span> /0.75/75% ✔</p>
<p><em>Do not accept 0.75% or 75 or ratios</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">d.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a.<em> O.s. indica</em> has more of the ancestral allele «for all three genes» ✔ <em>Accept converse</em>.</p>
<p>b. lower/higher values for ancestral/derived are not for the same genes ✔ <em>Allow specific gene examples</em>.</p>
<p>c. for <em>O. s. indica</em> the highest proportion is for <span style="text-decoration: underline;">DPL2</span> <span style="text-decoration: underline;">ancestral</span>, but for <em>O. s. japonica</em> is <span style="text-decoration: underline;">GS3</span> <span style="text-decoration: underline;">derived</span> allele ✔ <em>Allow converse for smallest derived.</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">e.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>any reference to comparison ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">f.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. large difference in diversity index between <em>O. s. indica</em> and <em>O. s. japonica</em> «suggests independent evolution» ✔</p>
<p>b. «some of the» peaks/troughs for <em>O. s. indica</em> and <em>O. s. japonica</em> in different positions «suggests independent evolution» ✔</p>
<p>c.<em> O.s. indica</em> has a similar diversity index to <em>O. rufipogon</em> «which suggests closer relationship/recent divergence» ✔<br/><em>Allow converse for japonica</em></p>
<p>d.<em> O.s. japonica</em> has very different proportions of ancestral and derived alleles compared to <em>O. s. indica</em> ✔</p>
<p>e.<em> O.s. indica</em> has similar large number of ancestral alleles to <em>O. rufipogon I/II</em> ✔ <em>Allow converse for derived</em></p>
<p>f. <em>O.s. japonica</em> has a large number of derived alleles similar to <em>O. rufipogon III</em><br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>«but» the number of derived alleles is greater in <em>O. s. japonica</em> than in <em>O.rufipogon III</em> ✔ <em>Allow converse for ancestral</em></p>
<p>g.<em> O.s. indica</em> and <em>O. s. japonica</em> are in different clades ✔<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/><em>O.s. indica</em> and <em>O. rufipogon</em> I are in the same clade ✔</p>
<p>h. evidence from one chromosome/3 genes/2 studies is not sufficient to form a conclusion ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">g.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/3/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","3"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19N.2.SL.TZ0.1"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","4-1-species-communities-and-ecosystems, 3-1-genes"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">d.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">e.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">f.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">g.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
